name: Question 30 @ 2022/2023

Question.content: Give the structure of the product of dehydrohalogenation of 2-bromopropane

Explanation.content:
Elimination reaction (Zaitsev's rule):

CH3-CH(Br)-CH3 + KOH(alc) → CH3-CH=CH2 + KBr + H2O

  • Removal of H and Br from adjacent carbons
  • Forms the more substituted alkene (propene)
  • Requires alcoholic KOH and heat

topic: Alkyl Halides: Substitution and Elimination Reactions

name: Question 28 @ 2017/2018

Question.content: Give the product of dehydrohalogenation of 2-bromopent-2-ene

Explanation.content:
• Reaction: CH3-CH=C(Br)-CH2-CH3 + base → CH2=CH-CH=CH-CH3 + HBr
  • Mechanism: E2 elimination (strong base removes H from β-carbon)
  • Forms conjugated diene (alternating double bonds)
  • Not 2-pentyne (would require different starting material)
  • Zaitsev's rule applies - forms more substituted double bond
